feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and
auto-implement steps with a recursive fs.readdirSync('lua') call.
This keeps codebase context up to date automatically as new Lua
modules are added without requiring workflow changes.

Previously only impact < 4 triggered the label. Now type=other always
gets 'evaluation needed' regardless of impact score, matching the
intended behaviour that unclassified issues always need manual review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

## Priority 1 — Watermark-based incremental processing
- Add `scan_cursors` table to FactsDB (scan_type, last_session_ts, last_run_at,
  sessions_processed) with `getScanCursor()` / `updateScanCursor()` methods
- `getSessionFilePathsSince`: add optional `sinceTimestamp` param for cursor-based filtering
- `gatherSessionFiles`: add `sinceTimestampMs` param for cursor-based watermark mode
- Make `extract-procedures`, `extract-directives`, `extract-reinforcement`,
  `distill`, and `self-correction-run` incremental by default (only sessions
  newer than last run)
- Add `--full` flag to all five commands to force full re-scan

## Priority 1.5 — Startup guards & concurrency locks (#288)
- `SCAN_IN_PROGRESS` in-memory map: skip if scan already running
- 23-hour guard: skip if last_run_at < 23h ago; log with hours-ago + hint
- `acquireScanSlot` / `clearScanLock` helpers used by all scan operations
- Guards applied to: extract-procedures, extract-directives,
  extract-reinforcement, distill, self-correction-run

## Priority 2 — Default all LLM models to nano/cheap tier (cost safety)
- Add `OPENAI_NANO_CRON_MODEL`, `GEMINI_NANO_MODEL`, `CLAUDE_NANO_MODEL` constants
- `getDefaultCronModelLegacy` + `getDefaultPreferredModelList`: return nano-tier
  models when tier="nano" and no explicit config (replaces OPENAI_DEFAULT fallback)
- `runExtractReinforcementForCli`: change default extractionTier "heavy" → "nano"
- `runSelfCorrectionRunForCli` spawn: change fallback "heavy" → "default"
- `runAnalyzeFeedbackPhrasesForCli`: change "heavy" → "default"
- `trajectory-analyze` LLM call: change "heavy" → "nano"

## Priority 3 — Fix config validation rejects valid fields (#289)
- Add missing fields to `selfCorrection` schema in `openclaw.plugin.json`:
  positiveRulesSection, reinforcementLLMAnalysis, reinforcementToProposals,
  agentsRuleToProposals (all were in TS type + parser but absent from JSON schema,
  causing `additionalProperties: false` to reject them)

Stage k6 summaries, counter metrics, and GC report in experiment commits

Update git add staging in Apply-Suggestion, Revert-ExperimentCode, and

- k6-summary*.json (p95 latency, RPS, error rates)

- gc-report.json (GC generation counts, pause times)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Gitignore raw profiling data; keep only agent analysis in PRs

Changes:
- Fix sample-api .gitignore: allow experiment-* dirs but ignore raw data
  (PerfView ETL/CSV, dotnet-counters, k6 JSON summaries) while keeping
  agent prompts/responses and analyzer reports
- Update root .gitignore documentation to reflect new policy
- Fix git add staging in Apply-Suggestion, Revert-ExperimentCode, and
  Invoke-HoneLoop to explicitly add only agent analysis files instead
  of the entire experiment directory
- Rename optimization-queue/log to experiment-queue/log across all
  harness scripts (Build-AnalysisContext, Manage-OptimizationQueue,
  Update-OptimizationMetadata, Apply-Suggestion, Revert-ExperimentCode)

Experiment PRs now contain:
- Code changes (the optimization)
- Agent prompts and responses (analysis, classification)
- Analyzer reports (cpu-hotspots, memory-gc prompt+response)
- Root cause analysis (root-cause.md)
- Metadata (experiment-queue, experiment-log, run-metadata.json)

Excluded from PRs:
- PerfView ETL/ZIP files (~100-150MB each)
- CPU stacks folded text, allocation type exports
- GC report JSON, dotnet-counters CSV/JSON
- k6 summary JSON files
- All collector stderr/export logs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add datetime timestamps to harness console output

All Write-Information calls now include [HH:mm:ss] prefix via Write-Status
helper function. Write-HoneLog.ps1 also prefixes timestamps. Decorative
lines (separators, blank lines) are excluded from timestamping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add MIT license

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add allocation type export and fix strict-mode crashes in analyzers

Export sampled allocation tick data from PerfView ETL using
SaveManagedHeapAllocStacksAsCsv. The data was being collected
(/DotNetAllocSampled) but never extracted  now exported as
alloc-types-folded.txt and passed via AllocTypesPath.

Fix strict-mode terminating errors in memory-gc and cpu-hotspots
analyzers: replace direct property access on hashtables (which
throws under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest) with ContainsKey
checks. Allocation data is now gracefully optional with a log
message when absent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden PerfView collectors for long runs

- Add /NoNGenPdbs to both collectors  skips NGEN PDB generation during
  merge (saves 4-10s per pass, not needed for folded stacks or GC stats)
- Add .etl.log.txt and .etl.zip.abort to stale file cleanup in both
  Start-Collector scripts  prevents false-positive completion detection
  from leftover files in interrupted prior runs
- Verify kill success in both Stop-Collector scripts  warn if PerfView
  process fails to terminate instead of silently continuing, preventing
  undetected orphaned process accumulation over long runs (100+ experiments)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix PerfView stop: poll log for completion instead of blind WaitForExit

PerfView /NoGui has a known bug where the process lingers after completing
all work (collection, rundown, merge, zip). Previously, Stop-Collector
blocked on WaitForExit for the full StopTimeoutSec (300s default), wasting
~5 minutes per collector pass even though data was ready in ~40s.

Now polls PerfView's log file every 3s for the '[DONE' completion marker.
Once detected:
- 5s grace period for natural exit
- Clean termination if still lingering (data confirmed safe)
- ETW session cleanup only needed in the true timeout fallback path

This reduces the stop phase from ~300s to ~45s per collector pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace requireContext() with safe context patterns in ProfilesFragment

Replace all requireContext() calls (except the prefs lazy init) with
'val ctx = context ?: return' to prevent IllegalStateException crashes
when the fragment is detached during lifecycle transitions.

- Add 'import android.content.Context'
- Pass ctx parameter to filterBtn() and buildPoolCard()
- Use safe bounds check (getOrNull) in setupProfileSpinner
- Use 'context ?: return@setOnClickListener' in click handlers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Clean up console output formatting: remove garbled box-drawing characters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix PerfView CPU merge failure: remove /ThreadTime, add /StackCompression:false

Remove /ThreadTime from PerfView CPU collector to avoid generating massive
context-switch traces (1.3GB ETL) that cause ETW merge failure 0x1069.
Add /StackCompression:false as the official workaround for the ETW compression
bug (microsoft/perfview#1281).

CPU sampling stacks, CLR events, and allocation data are all retained.

Add /focusProcess to PerfView collectors to fix CPU export failure

/ThreadTime creates 34K+ process entries in the ETL trace. The unfiltered
fallback also crashes on Idle process symbol resolution.

export to find the process by name. The PID is already available via the
-ProcessId parameter passed to Start-Collector.

commit.message:
Fix critical PerfView collector reliability issues

Three root causes for PerfView collection failures:

1. Stale ETW sessions after force-kill: When PerfView was killed by
   StopTimeoutSec, orphaned 'NT Kernel Logger' and 'PerfViewSession'
   ETW sessions blocked subsequent runs. Added logman cleanup in both
   Start-Collector (pre-flight) and Stop-Collector (post force-kill).

2. MaxCollectSec (90s) < k6 duration (120s): PerfView auto-stopped
   30s before the load test ended, missing the stress-load phase.
   Increased to 150s (120s k6 + 30s margin).

3. Dead alloc-types code in CPU export: Ran PerfView GCStats (up to
   300s timeout) to produce alloc-types.json that was always empty
   the code's own comment acknowledged 'GCStats does not export it'.
   Removed ~120 lines of dead code.

Also added stale intermediate file cleanup (.etl, .kernel.etl, etc.)
before starting collection to prevent conflicts from interrupted runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add configurable timeout to PerfView export commands

The Export-CollectorData.ps1 scripts for both perfview-cpu and
perfview-gc used Start-Process -Wait with no timeout, causing
the harness to hang indefinitely when PerfView UserCommands
stalled on large /ThreadTime ETL files.

Replace all -Wait calls with WaitForExit(timeout) + force-kill,
matching the pattern already used in Stop-Collector.ps1.

New ExportTimeoutSec setting (default 300s) added to:
- collector.psd1 DefaultSettings for both collectors
- config.psd1 CollectorSettings overrides

commit.message:
chore: add Lua-specific Copilot code review instructions (#5)

Add .github/instructions/lua.instructions.md scoped to **/*.lua with
applyTo front matter so GitHub Copilot applies it automatically during
code review. Covers six areas:

- Simplicity and minimal changes: flag over-engineering, prefer early
  returns over deep nesting, change only what the task requires.
- No redundant guards: one check per invariant per execution path,
  trust established nil/validity invariants downstream.
- Execution path coverage: every branch reachable, async coroutines
  must not be silently abandoned on error paths.
- LuaDoc annotations: @param/@return on all public M.* functions,
  @type on class-shaped returns, @async on plenary-wrapped functions,
  types.lua as single source of type truth.
- Idiomatic Neovim Lua: prefer vim.api.* over deprecated vim.fn
  option APIs, use vim.system() for subprocesses, correct ipairs/pairs
  usage, table.concat over .. in loops, strict M module pattern.
- Performance: no shell-outs in loops/events, bulk buffer reads,
  local-variable caching in loops, nvim_chan_send for terminal I/O,
  flag O(n²) patterns, validate before assuming impact.

Also extend .github/copilot-instructions.md with a working guidelines
section covering Lua code change rules, git workflow conventions, and
a revised PR description format: formatted with subtitles, bullet
points for lists, and code samples where applicable.

commit.message:
Charts: Fix composition legend position and alignment (#47478)

* fix(charts): Respect legend position in composition API

Composition legends (<Chart.Legend position="top" />) always appeared
at the bottom because they were rendered in the children slot, which
comes after the chart in the Stack layout. Alignment and orientation
worked since they affect Legend styling; position controls layout
placement and was ignored.

Extend useChartChildren to extract Legend children, read their position
prop, and return legendChildren. Charts now render legendChildren in
the top or bottom slot based on position, so composition legends honor
position="top" and position="bottom" across LineChart, BarChart,
PieChart, PieSemiCircleChart, and LeaderboardChart.

Fixes CHARTS-91.

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix args on LineChart story

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(charts): Remove unreleased changelog entry

Manual edits to the changelog are not the way.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Reuse LegendProps position type in useChartChildren

LegendChild duplicated the Legend component's position type ('top' | 'bottom'). Using NonNullable<LegendProps['position']> keeps the type in sync with the Legend component and avoids drift when position options change (e.g. future 'left' | 'right' support noted in Legend types).

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove obsolete useHasLegendChild hook

useChartChildren now extracts Legend components with position support
for alignment options. Charts derive "has legend" from
legendChildren.length instead of a separate hook call, so
useHasLegendChild became redundant. Removing it shrinks the public
API and keeps legend detection in one place.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add changelog entries.

* refactor(charts): Extract renderLegendSlot and add LegendPosition type

Each chart duplicated the same filter/map logic to render legend children by position. renderLegendSlot centralizes this.

LegendPosition provides a single source of truth for 'top' | 'bottom'
across the legend component, chart props, and composition hooks,
avoiding drift when adding 'left' | 'right' support later.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Remove duplicate Legend extraction tests from useChartChildren

Tests at lines 67–88 duplicated tests at 159–177 (default position /
position top). Collapse into the more comprehensive set at 159–191,
which also covers multiple legends.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(charts): Correct PR 47478 changelog scope and significance

The github-actions bot incorrectly added changelog entries to all
plugins that depend on charts. Only the charts package was modified;
plugins merely consume the fix. Removed the eight incorrect plugin
entries (backup, boost, jetpack, protect, search, social,
starter-plugin, videopress).

Updated the charts entry to significance: minor and type: changed.
The Legend position fix is a patch-level bugfix, but removing
useHasLegendChild from @automattic/charts/hooks is a breaking API
change. Pre-1.0, we treat breaking changes as minor bumps.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant LegendPosition cast in useChartChildren

The position value (child.props?.position ?? 'bottom') already satisfies
LegendPosition. The explicit cast was unnecessary since TypeScript infers
the type from the default. Minor formatting tweaks.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Wire legend props to Storybook args in composition story

WithCompositionLegend lacked control over orientation, alignment, and position,
so users couldn't interactively explore composition legend layouts. Binding
these props to story args makes the story more demonstrative.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Simplify legend alignment SCSS structure

Alignment was duplicated under each position (top/bottom), and vertical vs
horizontal legends use different axes (align-items vs justify-content).
Flattening the structure: alignment at root level, vertical overrides use
align-items, horizontal uses justify-content. Add align-self: stretch so
legend spans container width.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove unused legend position styles

The position-top and position-bottom classes only set position: relative
with no layout effect. Drop the classes and their styles to reduce
unused CSS. Keep position prop for composition API slot placement.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Clarify legend position prop and fix bar chart example

Position controls slot placement in the composition API, not visual styling.
Update JSDoc and API docs to reflect this. Fix BarChart docs example:
legendOrientation is not a valid prop; use orientation instead.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Add unit tests for renderLegendSlot

Covers empty input, position filtering, key generation, and ordering.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove unused position prop from BaseLegend

Position is read by chart composition from child.props for slot placement;
BaseLegend never used it. Dropping the param and eslint-disable.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Simplify LeaderboardChart empty state layout

Remove legend slots from the empty state — legends add noise when there is
no data. Drop redundant composition API comments.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use explicit bottom check for PieSemiCircle legend

legendPosition !== 'top' was equivalent but obscured intent. Match
LeaderboardChart pattern for consistency.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Default invalid legend position to bottom

Only 'top' and 'bottom' are valid. Invalid values (e.g. 'left' passed
before implementation) now fall back to 'bottom' instead of rendering
in wrong slots.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Rename otherChildren to nonLegendChildren

Clearer name for children excluding Legend. Add nonLegendChildren
returning all non-Legend children in original order (otherChildren
categorized by compound type). Update BarChart, LineChart, LeaderboardChart.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Add composition legend tests for all chart types

Verify composition Legend renders as child, ignores showLegend prop,
and respects position (top vs bottom) across BarChart, LineChart,
PieChart, and PieSemiCircleChart.

Made-with: Cursor

commit.message:
Fix CPU stack export failure and false success reporting

PerfView's SaveCPUStacksAsCsv fails with 'Could not find process' when
/ThreadTime tracing creates 29K+ process entries that corrupt the
TraceLog.Processes index.

Changes:
- Add fallback: retry SaveCPUStacksAsCsv without process filter when the
  filtered attempt produces no CSV, then exclude unrelated modules (k6,
  conhost, etc.) during CSV parsing
- Delete stale CSV before each invocation to detect fresh output
- Return accurate Success flag based on actual stacks found, not
  Test-Path on a placeholder file
- Fix GC HTML process section matching to handle PID-only headings
  (perfview-gc and perfview-cpu allocation export)
- Document that GCStats HTML never includes Allocation Tick tables
  even with /DotNetAllocSampled; allocation type data requires a
  different export approach

Tested against experiment-1 ETL: produces 50 real CPU stacks (was 0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Daily Builder: fix(installer): increase ArgoCD and VCluster install timeouts (#2909)

* fix(installer): increase ArgoCD and VCluster install timeouts

ArgoCD was timing out in VCluster environments with layered stacks
(Calico + Gatekeeper + ArgoCD). Root cause analysis:

- GetInstallTimeout returned DefaultInstallTimeout (5 min) for VCluster
- helmInstallerFactory applied max(5min, 10min) = 10 min Helm timeout
- ArgoCD installer added 5 min ContextTimeoutBuffer = 15 min context deadline
- CI failure log showed exactly 15 min elapsed before 'context deadline exceeded'

Fixes:
1. Add VClusterInstallTimeout = 8 min as the base timeout for VCluster
   distributions, accounting for the ~20-30% syncer/webhook overhead
2. Increase ArgoCDInstallTimeout from 10 → 20 min so that ArgoCD installs
   in complex stacks (CNI + policy engine + ArgoCD) have sufficient headroom
3. Add VCluster case to GetInstallTimeout so all components benefit
4. Add tests for the new constant and VCluster distribution timeout path

With these changes, ArgoCD on VCluster gets:
  max(8 min VCluster base, 20 min ArgoCD min) = 20 min Helm timeout
  + 5 min ContextTimeoutBuffer = 25 min total context deadline

Fixes: https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/issues/2899

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[docs] docs: document Vanilla CSI N/A behavior in update table (#2908)

* docs: document Vanilla CSI N/A update behavior

Kind bundles local-path-provisioner by default, so the KSail detector
cannot distinguish Kind's bundled CSI from a KSail-installed CSI driver.
The diff engine skips CSI comparison for Vanilla (and VCluster) entirely
to prevent false-positive diffs — CSI config changes have no effect
during `ksail cluster update` for Vanilla clusters.

Update the support-matrix Update Behavior table to reflect this:
- Change Vanilla CSI from 'In-place' to 'N/A²'
- Add footnote ² with a clear explanation and workaround (recreate cluster)

Follows the same fix applied in #2855 to resolve false-positive diffs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
